_comment: Firebase Realtime Database security rules for Navigator Algo. Copy the 'rules' object into Firebase console → Realtime Database → Rules. DO NOT ship without these — without them, anyone with the DB URL can read every provider's data and mint fake licenses.

admins._comment: Admin allowlist. Set /admins/{uid} = true in Firebase console for each admin. Admins can mint new provider IDs + list all providers. Regular users can only check their own admin status (used to conditionally show the Admin nav link).

users_by_email._comment: Email -> uid index. Written by the user's own client on sign-in (touchUserProfile) so admins can look up a user's UID by email at provider-bind time without needing Admin SDK in the browser. Key is the email, lowercased, with '.' replaced by ',' (RTDB forbids '.' in keys).

$email_key..write: auth != null && newData.val() === auth.uid && (!data.exists() || data.val() === auth.uid || root.child('admins').child(auth.uid).val() === true)

providers._comment: Admins can list all providers (for the admin mint page). Regular users fall through to per-$pid rules where they can only read their own claimed provider.

issued_rec_licenses._comment: Per-provider index of REC- codes this provider has minted from their dashboard (see /rec_licenses/{code} for the full record). Value is always true — this is just a lookup list so the provider dashboard can list its own paid licenses without needing read access to the global /rec_licenses tree.

signals._comment: Provider-owned signal stream. Receivers (EAs) read via the eaRead Cloud Function (service-account, bypasses rules) which performs license validation; direct client reads are restricted to the provider owner + admins so one provider cannot scrape another provider's signals. Providers write via eaWrite (service account), not directly.

stats._comment: Aggregated counters for dashboard; written by Cloud Functions.

licenses._comment: Individual license nodes. Receivers validate their key by reading /licenses/{code}. Write access restricted to admin/service account.

rec_licenses._comment: Paid Pro-Receiver licenses (REC-XXXXXXXX). One node per purchased Pro Receiver on MQL5 Market. Unlike SUB- codes (which live under /providers/{pid}/sub_licenses and consume a provider's free slot), REC- codes are user-owned, time-limited, and independent of any provider's 10-slot cap. Two mint paths: (a) admin-minted from /admin.html (for MQL5 Market buyers) — following_pid stays null until first activation; (b) provider-minted from the provider dashboard — following_pid is pre-pinned to the minting provider at creation (see created_by_pid). Cloud Functions read here when an EA presents a REC- prefixed code.

$code._comment: Per-code rules grant narrower access than the collection-level .read/.write: a provider can read + mint + revoke codes they own (created_by_pid matches a provider whose owner_uid is auth.uid), scoped so they cannot touch other providers' codes or admin-minted codes.
$code..write: auth != null && (root.child('admins').child(auth.uid).val() === true || (!data.exists() && newData.child('created_by_pid').exists() && root.child('providers').child(newData.child('created_by_pid').val()).child('owner_uid').val() === auth.uid && newData.child('created_by_uid').val() === auth.uid && newData.child('following_pid').val() === newData.child('created_by_pid').val()) || (data.exists() && data.child('created_by_pid').exists() && root.child('providers').child(data.child('created_by_pid').val()).child('owner_uid').val() === auth.uid))

deleted_providers._comment: Archive of providers deleted from the admin console. Admins can list/read to undo; nothing else has access. Manual cleanup after ~30 days (later PR: automated trim).

public_providers._comment: Optional: per-provider public stats page at navigatoralgo.com/p/{provider_id}. Stores only non-sensitive fields (display_name, active_copiers count, total_signals). Populated by a Cloud Function scheduled job.

system._comment: System-wide announcements. /system/notice is a single node that renders a maintenance/status banner across admin + provider dashboard. Readable by any authed user so EAs and pages can both see it; writable by admins only.
